text: IDF EVACUATION ORDERS: Col. Adraee issued evac warnings for ~12 southern Lebanon towns — Jibchit, Sarafand, Kafra, Al-Sharqiyah and more. Civilians ordered 1km+ evacuation. Signals imminent large-scale IDF operations. IDF Chief Zamir: 'there is no ceasefire' in south Lebanon.
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed IDF spokesman Col. Avichay Adraee issued evacuation warnings for ~12 towns.

text: RUBIO REJECTS IRAN PROPOSAL: 'Nuclear question is the reason we're in this.' Questions whether submitter had authority. Mocks Hormuz terms: 'Yes, the straits are open, as long as you coordinate with Iran, get our permission, or we'll blow you up, and you pay us.' No diplomatic off-ramp visible.
verificationNote: The Hill, Times of Israel, ABC News confirmed Rubio's detailed rejection.

text: HEZBOLLAH vs IDF: Rockets and explosive drones fired at IDF troops in S. Lebanon May 3 — 1 rocket intercepted, no Israeli injuries. IDF responded with counter-strikes on Hezbollah launch sites. Despite 'ceasefire,' fighting continues daily. CNN publishes major analysis on Hezbollah fiber-optic FPV drones — 15km cable, undetectable.
verificationNote: Times of Israel and CNN confirmed rocket/drone attacks and fiber-optic drone capability report.

text: IRAN RESPONSE: Deputy FM Gharibabadi says 'ball is in US court — prepared for both paths.' Netanyahu convenes security cabinet to discuss Iran + potential Gaza return. Hundreds protest in Tel Aviv against 'government of debacle and disaster.' Lavrov-Araghchi call discussed war prospects and Hormuz.
verificationNote: Manila Times, PressTV, Times of Israel confirmed Gharibabadi statement, Netanyahu cabinet, and Tel Aviv protests.

text: GLOBAL MOVES: Jordan strikes drug/weapons storage in Syria's Sweida province. Von der Leyen warns Iran war consequences 'may echo for years.' China's Xi calls for Hormuz reopening and ceasefire. Russia benefits from oil prices — KSE estimates $45-151B extra Russian revenue in 2026.
verificationNote: Arab News, Bloomberg, The Hill, PIIE confirmed Jordan strikes, EU warning, China diplomacy, Russia oil windfall.

text: DAY 65 LATE PM ROUNDUP: IDF evacuation orders ~12 Lebanon towns. Rubio rejects Iran proposal — 'nuclear question.' Trump: 'if they misbehave.' Gharibabadi: 'ball in US court.' Hezbollah rockets at IDF (no injuries). CNN: fiber-optic drones. Netanyahu security cabinet. Jordan strikes Syria. Brent $111.25 (+39.3%). Gas $4.433. Hormuz Day 62.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, The Hill, Al Jazeera, CNN, Manila Times, Arab News — all cross-verified.

text: DAY 64 PM UPDATE: IDF soldier killed + 15 wounded by Hezbollah drones. ~50 IDF airstrikes S. Lebanon (7+ killed). Ford carrier departs. Dark Eagle hypersonic requested. Khamenei: US 'disgraceful defeat.' Iran military: war 'likely' to restart. Senate rejects war powers 50-47. Gas $4.433. NASDAQ 25,114 record. Rial 1.776M/USD. 61% say war was mistake.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, The National, Bloomberg, Middle East Eye, Time, AAA, WaPo — all cross-verified.

text: TRUMP PIRATES: Florida rally — Trump openly describes US Navy seizing Iranian vessels as piracy: 'We took over the ship, took over the cargo, took over the oil. It's a very profitable business... We're like pirates.' Direct contradiction of War Powers letter that hostilities have 'terminated.' Adds legal exposure to ongoing blockade.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera and TIME confirmed Trump's 'pirates' remarks at Florida rally May 2.

text: DAMASCUS HIT: Israeli aircraft struck near Syrian Presidential Palace and across Suwayda/Daraa governorates early May 2. Syrian MoH: 3 killed, 34 injured. Netanyahu and Katz issued joint statement warning Syrian regime against threats to Druze community. SOHR later reported 20+ strikes including al-Tha'lah Military Airbase in Suwayda.
verificationNote: TIME confirmed Damascus area strikes and Druze warning. SOHR cited via multiple outlets.

text: DAMAGE ASSESSMENT: PressTV report — 16 US military sites in 8 countries damaged by Iranian/proxy strikes; some 'virtually inoperable.' Estimated $800M+ damage in first 2 weeks alone. Affected: Al Udeid (Qatar), Ali Al Salem (Kuwait), Al Dhafra (UAE), 5th Fleet HQ (Bahrain), Prince Sultan (Saudi Arabia), Erbil (Iraq). Corroborates earlier CNN/NBC investigations.
verificationNote: The Hill, GlobalSecurity, PressTV all reported assessment. Iranian state-source so 'likely' rather than 'confirmed.'

text: MARITIME FREEDOM CONSTRUCT: State Dept and Pentagon launch new coalition to coordinate partner nation action and information-sharing for safe Hormuz transit. Counter to Iran's 'toll' demands. CENTCOM total: 48 Iranian vessels turned around in 20 days. Hormuz traffic at ~5% of pre-war (2,000 ships stranded).
verificationNote: Al Jazeera and CBS News liveblog confirmed Maritime Freedom Construct and 48-vessel CENTCOM tally.

text: CONVENT STRIKE: IDF demolishes Catholic convent in Majdal Selm, S. Lebanon. Likely to draw Vatican condemnation given Pope Leo's prior 'delusion of omnipotence' remarks. Lebanon DT updated by Health Ministry to 2,659 killed/8,183 injured. Hezbollah suicide drones near Bint Jbeil (Moussa Abbas complex), Hula village, Biyyada.
verificationNote: Voice of Emirates, Washington Post, Al Jazeera confirmed Majdal Selm convent demolition and Lebanon DT update.

text: DAY 64 LATE PM: Trump 'pirates' Florida rally. Israeli strikes Damascus area + Syrian palace; 3 killed/34 injured (Syrian MoH). Catholic convent demolished Majdal Selm. PressTV: 16 US bases damaged $800M+. Pezeshkian: blockade 'intolerable' Persian Gulf Day. Lebanon DT 2,659/8,183. Iran MoH 3,375/26,500+. Maritime Freedom Construct launched. Brent $108. Gas $4.433. Hormuz Day 61.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, TIME, The Hill, Voice of Emirates, Pajhwok, BSS News, AAA — all cross-verified.

text: IRGC WARNING: Deputy commander Asadi says war with US "likely" to resume ��� "evidence shows US not committed to agreements." Aerospace Mousavi warns: "We will see the same thing happen to your warships." Significant escalatory rhetoric after ceasefire period.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera liveblog, Express Tribune, Jerusalem Post all confirmed IRGC statements May 2.

text: ARMS BYPASS: Rubio invokes emergency under Arms Export Control Act for $8.6B in sales — Qatar $4.01B Patriot, Kuwait $2.5B battle command, Israel/UAE APKWS. Third congressional bypass of conflict. Senate rejected War Powers Resolution 50-47 (Collins broke from GOP).
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Bloomberg, The Hill all confirmed emergency arms sales May 1-2.

text: LEBANON UPDATE: 41 killed in 24h of Israeli airstrikes May 2. IDF claims destruction of ~120 Hezbollah sites over weekend (70 military, 50 infrastructure). White phosphorus allegations near Seriane/Taybeh, Marjayoun district. Hezbollah issues 5 ceasefire conditions.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, NBC News, The National, Times of Israel confirmed Lebanon casualties and IDF operations.

text: BLOCKADE ECONOMICS: Pentagon says blockade cost Iran $4.8B — 31 tankers/53M barrels stuck in Gulf. Iran oil exports collapsed 70% from 1.85M to 567K bpd. Only 12-24 days onshore storage left. OFAC warns shipping firms against paying Iran Hormuz tolls ($1M+/ship). Treasury sanctions Hengli Petrochemical + 40 ships.
verificationNote: Gulf News, Fortune/Bloomberg, US News, Treasury press release confirmed blockade economics.

text: CORPORATE CASUALTY: Spirit Airlines ceases operations — first US corporate casualty of Iran war. Jet fuel doubled from $2.24 to $4.51/gal. Failed $500M federal bailout bid. Pentagon withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany over Merz "humiliated" comments. Trump threatens Italy/Spain troop cuts too.
verificationNote: Time, NPR, Washington Post, Military Times confirmed Spirit shutdown and Germany troop withdrawal.

text: DAY 65 ROUNDUP: IRGC warns war "likely" to resume. IDF destroys 120 Hezbollah sites; 41 killed 24h Lebanon. Trump "hostilities terminated" letter. $8.6B arms bypass. Iran 14-point proposal rejected. Blockade cost Iran $4.8B. Spirit Airlines folds. Hengli sanctions. Jordan strikes Syria. Netanyahu security cabinet. Brent $111.25 (+39.3%). Gas $4.433. Hormuz Day 62.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, NPR, PBS, Times of Israel, CNN, Fortune, Gulf News, PressTV, Time, Military Times — all cross-verified.

text: NETANYAHU FIGHTER JETS: Israel to purchase 2 new squadrons — F-35 fleet doubles 50→100, F-15IA 25→50. Also orders 'special project' to counter Hezbollah fiber-optic drones that killed Sgt. Fooks. Pledges NIS 350B (~$118B) defense independence plan. 'Our pilots can reach anywhere in Iranian airspace.'
verificationNote: Israel Hayom and Jerusalem Post confirmed fighter jet purchase announcement and counter-drone project.

text: IRGC ULTIMATUM: Intelligence department sends Pentagon deadline to lift naval blockade. 'Trump must choose between impossible military operation or bad deal with Islamic Republic.' Brig. Gen. Mousavi: 'Your warships will face same fate' as US bases. Forces on 'full standby' for war resumption.
verificationNote: Middle East Monitor and Al Jazeera confirmed IRGC ultimatum and Mousavi warning.

text: CEASEFIRE MONITORING CRISIS: Lebanon army chief Gen. Haykal holds emergency meeting with US Gen. Clearfield in Beirut. Israel issued new evacuation orders for 11 villages beyond occupied areas. IDF expanding operations into Nabatieh district despite declared ceasefire. CNN: 'Two months in, almost everybody is a loser.'
verificationNote: The National, Al Jazeera, Free Malaysia Today confirmed generals meeting and evacuation orders.

text: FERTILIZER CRISIS: Euronews reports urea at $698/tonne (+47% from pre-war). Half of global urea transits Hormuz. 25% of US farmers haven't purchased fertilizer for planting. USDA food-at-home +3.6% forecast. WFP: 45M additional food-insecure by year-end if conflict continues.
verificationNote: Euronews fertilizer report, USDA food price outlook, and IFPRI food security analysis all confirmed.

text: DAY 65 AM PT UPDATE: Netanyahu 2 fighter squadrons (F-35→100, F-15IA→50) + counter-drone. IRGC blockade deadline. Lebanon generals emergency meet. WaPo poll: Iraq/Vietnam disapproval. Gas $4.446 new wartime high. Fertilizer crisis: urea +47%. Iran rial 1.81M/USD. Hormuz Day 62. Blockade Day 21.
verificationNote: Israel Hayom, Middle East Monitor, The National, AAA, Euronews, WaPo — all cross-verified.
